name: Pakora Batter
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the chick pea flour and ghee. Mix well.
2. Add lemon juice, cayenne pepper, turmeric, garam masala, coriander, and salt. Stir until smooth.
3. Gradually add cold water while mixing with a fork or whisk until the batter is smooth and free of lumps.
4. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
5. Cover the bowl and let the batter rest for 10 to 15 minutes.
6. Beat the batter again with a fork to lighten it, then stir in baking powder (if using).
7. Prepare pakoras by cutting vegetables into desired shapes:
- Cauliflower florets
- Eggplant slices (1/4-inch thick)
- Potatoes or yams slices (1/8-inch thick)
- Bell peppers, strips (1/4-inch thick)
- Zucchini slices (diagonal, 1/4-inch thick)
- Asparagus tips, blanched and dried
8. Heat ghee in a wok or deep skillet to about 350°F (175°C).
9. Dip each vegetable piece into the batter, then carefully lower it into hot oil.
10. Fry pakoras until golden brown on all sides, turning occasionally.
11. Remove with tongs and drain on paper towels.
12. Serve immediately.
